specific_location: On the ice at Valmont Reservoir.
description: First, my trip report, including a brief written description of the species, can be found here: http://tiny.cc/OXM2U  A large, pale-dusky gull. About the same size as nearby Herring Gulls, although appearing huskier and chunkier in overall jizz.  The bird was rather uniformly colored overall, paler than nearby first-cycle Herring Gulls. Its tail and primary tips were about the same shade of light brown as the rest of the bird--they were about the color of coffee with a lot of milk stirred in. The wings were relatively short, not extending very much beyond the tail. On the primary tips on the bird at rest, I was able to see slight variation in shading, but it was not substantial.  The bird's bill was mainly or entirely dark, and it was fairly large. Given its distance, I can't swear that the bill was entirely dark, but it was at least mainly dark.Behaviors: Standing on the ice with many other gulls.Call: None heard.Plumage: first-cycle
